checks.a_analysis: The output document faithfully represents the text found in the source PDF. No invented section headings, body text, or conclusions were discovered. The meeting minutes, agenda items, committee goals, and resignation letter content all correspond directly to the source material.

Mailing addresses provided in the resignation letter (Page 5) were cross-checked. The output correctly reflects '1528 Prison Camp Rd, Williamston, NC 27892' and 'PO Box 17209, Raleigh, NC 27619' as seen in the source document. No fabricated contact details like phone numbers or URLs were added.

All seven pages of the source PDF are represented in the output JSON. The agenda, draft minutes (including attendees and detailed points), roll call vote table, resignation letter, committee goals, and committee dates are all present in their respective sections.

The tables in the output correspond to the source tables. The agenda table (Page 1) has 10 data rows. The Roll Call Vote table (Page 4) includes all committee members. The Attendance table (Page 2), while reformatted into a key-value structure, includes all 23 individuals (plus the empty placeholder row) listed in the source matrix, accurately reflecting their attendance status based on the checkboxes.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Sections are generally complete. The numbered points in the minutes (Page 3) and the itemized goals (Page 6) and dates (Page 7) are all preserved. The omission of the date and time from the Page 2 table header is minor and not significant enough to fail this check.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: In the 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 Standards Committee Attendance' table on Page 2, the source header includes the specific date and time 'January 7, 2021 10:00 AM – Noon'. The output table caption omits this information, which is a discrepancy from the source content, even though the main document header correctly identifies the meeting date as October 7.
checks.b_evidence_source: NORTH CAROLINA 911 Standards Committee Attendance January 7, 2021 10:00 AM – Noon

checks.a_analysis: The conversion result for Section 2 (Page 2 of the source) contains two image elements (indices 14 and 15) which are identical in content (base64 string) and location. The source PDF for Page 2 contains only one NC DIT logo at the top. This constitutes a duplication of content in the converted output.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 contains a single logo image at the top.
checks.b_evidence_output: Elements at index 14 and 15 in the JSON are duplicate image objects referencing the same logo graphic.

checks.a_analysis: The heading hierarchy is preserved and logically structured. Section 1 uses H1 for the board and H2 for the agenda. Section 2 follows a similar H1/H2 structure for the board and committee name. Section 6 correctly identifies the main heading as H1 and the specific year goals as H2. The hierarchy is consistent and supports navigation.

The 'NORTH CAROLINA 911 Standards Committee Attendance' table (Element 13) has undergone a significant structural transformation that corrupts its original matrix-style organization. In the source (Page 2), the table has four distinct columns: 'Committee Members', 'In Person', 'Via Teams', and 'Not Present'. The conversion result flattens this into a two-column 'Field/Value' structure where the column headers from the source are merged into the text string of the 'Field' column (e.g., 'Commissioner Marty Cooke - In Person'). This is a corruption of the source table's structural organization.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2 attendance table with 4 columns: Name, In Person (checkbox), Via Teams (checkbox), Not Present (checkbox).
checks.b_evidence_output: Element index 13: a 2-column table with 'Field' and 'Value' headers where data from source columns 2-4 is merged into the 'Field' column strings.

checks.a_analysis: The overall reading order matches the logical flow of the source document. The sections follow the page order, and elements within sections generally follow the vertical flow of the PDF. Images are placed at the end of sections, which is a common accessibility adjustment and does not materially impact the flow of information.

Several numbered lists in the source document have been converted to unordered lists in the JSON. Specifically, the 'Meeting Minutes' on Page 3 (numbered 1-11) and the 'Standards Committee Goals' on Page 6 (numbered 1-3 for both 2021 and 2022) are all marked as 'list_type': 'unordered'. This loses the specific numbering semantics present in the source.
checks.b_evidence_output: Element index 17 ('list_type': 'unordered'), element index 32 ('list_type': 'unordered'), element index 34 ('list_type': 'unordered').

checks.a_analysis: No missing or fabricated links were identified. The document contains addresses and contact names but no explicit URLs or email links that the pipeline failed to capture or incorrectly injected.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document faithfully preserves the visual organization of the source PDF. The sequence of sections (Agenda, Attendance, Minutes, Vote, Resignation, Goals, Dates) follows the original page order exactly. While the grid layouts of the Attendance and Vote tables are transformed into accessible HTML tables (flattening the Attendance grid into a key-value list), the information hierarchy and visual flow remain clear and consistent with the source's intent. Responsive layout and navigation elements are expected differences.

All tables in the document are rendered correctly in HTML. The Agenda table (PDF p1), the Attendance table (PDF p2), and the Roll Call Vote table (PDF p4) are all converted into readable HTML tables with distinct headers and aligned columns/rows. Specifically, the complex Attendance grid with checkboxes has been successfully converted into a two-column accessible table that explicitly states 'true' or 'false' for each person's attendance status, ensuring the data is readable and understandable for all users. Headers are visually distinct from body content.

Meaningful images from the source PDF, specifically the organizational logos (NCDIT and NC 911 Board), are present in the HTML output. The logos appear in the header and throughout the body as expected. The signature image in the resignation letter (PDF p5) is replaced by text, which is a standard accessibility practice to ensure the name is machine-readable; given the printed name is immediately adjacent, this does not represent a loss of meaningful content.

There is no visual duplication of body content within the HTML document. While headings and the document title appear in the table of contents at the top and again in the body, this is an intentional and standard accessibility feature for navigation. The body content itself—paragraphs, tables, and lists—is rendered exactly once in the appropriate sections.
